user.title: Server connection
user.description: Set up the basic connection to your Gotify server.

data_description.name: Freely selectable name of this Home Assistant entry.
data_description.url: Base URL of your Gotify server, for example `https://gotify.example.com`.
data_description.verify_ssl: Disable this only if you intentionally use a self-signed or otherwise untrusted certificate.

access.title: Access and permissions
access.description: Select how this entry should be used and provide the matching token types.

data_description.mode: Full = send + receive, send only = notifications only, receive only = stream and management only.
data_description.app_token: Required for sending notifications from Home Assistant to Gotify.
data_description.client_token: Required for user, application, message and WebSocket access.

behavior.title: Update and streaming behavior
behavior.description: Define how often Home Assistant should poll Gotify and whether live messages should be received over WebSocket.

data_description.scan_interval: Lower values update sensors and statistics more frequently but create more API traffic.
data_description.enable_websocket: Recommended for incoming messages and live updates.

application.title: Monitored application
application.description: Optionally choose a Gotify application whose message list should be used for message statistics and bulk deletion.

data_description.monitored_application_id: Leave empty if no specific application should be monitored.

init.title: Behavior
init.description: Adjust connection verification, polling interval and live streaming.

data_description.verify_ssl: Disable this only if you intentionally use a self-signed or otherwise untrusted certificate.
data_description.scan_interval: Lower values update sensors and statistics more frequently but create more API traffic.
data_description.enable_websocket: Recommended for incoming messages and live updates.

application.title: Monitored application
application.description: Optionally choose which Gotify application should be monitored for message statistics and clear actions.

data_description.monitored_application_id: Leave empty if no specific application should be monitored.

send_message.name: Send message
send_message.description: Send a message through the selected Gotify entry.

entry_id.name: Entry ID
entry_id.description: Target a specific Gotify entry when multiple entries exist.

markdown.name: Markdown
markdown.description: Send the message as Markdown via `client::display.contentType`.

click_url.name: Click URL
click_url.description: URL to open when the notification is tapped.

big_image_url.name: Big image URL
big_image_url.description: Large image URL for clients that support it.

intent_url.name: Intent URL
intent_url.description: Android intent URL for the `onReceive` action.

extras.name: Extras
extras.description: Raw `extras` object that should be passed to Gotify unchanged.

delete_message.name: Delete message
delete_message.description: Delete a Gotify message by its numeric ID.

entry_id.name: Entry ID
entry_id.description: Target a specific Gotify entry when multiple entries exist.

clear_messages.name: Clear messages
clear_messages.description: Delete all messages belonging to the configured monitored application.

entry_id.name: Entry ID
entry_id.description: Target a specific Gotify entry when multiple entries exist.

refresh.name: Refresh
refresh.description: Refresh all Gotify data for the selected entry immediately.

entry_id.name: Entry ID
entry_id.description: Target a specific Gotify entry when multiple entries exist.

entry_id_required.message: Multiple Gotify entries are configured. Please specify an entry ID.

missing_app_token.message: This Gotify entry has no application token and cannot send messages.

missing_client_token.message: This Gotify entry has no client token and cannot manage or receive messages.

missing_monitored_application.message: No monitored application is configured for this Gotify entry.
